symptoms.symptom_name: Pain out of proportion to the visible injury, unrelieved by simple analgesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The earliest and most sensitive clinical feature of necrotising deep soft-tissue infection.
Simple cellulitis pain is usually proportionate to the visible inflammation.
Pain out of proportion also characterises compartment syndrome — another limb-threatening surgical emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly spreading erythema and swelling advancing beyond a marked margin within hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rate of spread beyond a marked border is faster than ordinary cellulitis and signals an aggressive fascial process.
Erythema occurs in cellulitis too, but not at this pace with systemic collapse.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemorrhagic bullae and dusky grey-purple skin necrosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemorrhagic bullae with skin necrosis are hallmark late signs of full-thickness fascial and vascular destruction.
Their presence effectively excludes simple cellulitis as the whole story.

symptoms.symptom_name: Subcutaneous crepitus (gas in the soft tissues) on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable crepitus indicates gas-forming organisms in the fascia — near-pathognomonic; a clinician-only finding, sealed and not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Paradoxical cutaneous anaesthesia over the necrotic area

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of sensation from destruction of cutaneous nerves is highly suggestive; requires clinician neurological examination, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: High fever with rigors and profound malaise

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic toxicity marks progression to sepsis and separates this from a bland DVT.
High fever and rigors are not features of an uncomplicated DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Confusion/drowsiness, diaphoresis and tachycardia (septic shock physiology)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Altered mentation with sweating and racing heart in the context of spreading infection indicates established septic shock — caregiver-reportable in telehealth.

Objective vital-sign derangement confirms septic shock — sealed clinician/device measurement; the AI Doctor infers circulatory compromise from the reported confusion, sweating and racing heart, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT/MRI showing fascial thickening and gas within the subcutaneous planes

investigation_details.investigation_name: CT or MRI of the affected limb
investigation_details.expected_result: Extensive fascial thickening with gas tracking in the subcutaneous/fascial planes
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Imaging must NOT delay surgery when suspicion is high; it is confirmatory, not a gatekeeper.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fascial gas on cross-sectional imaging is diagnostic — a hospital investigation, never available in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Pain out of proportion + rapidly spreading erythema + haemorrhagic bullae + subcutaneous crepitus together are as close to definitive as clinical signs get for necrotising fasciitis.
Fever plus altered mentation, sweating, tachycardia and hypotension establish septic shock complicating the source infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate emergency surgical assessment (definitive — surgical exploration and debridement)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive diagnostic and therapeutic step; time-critical. In telehealth this means activating 000 for transport to surgical care, not ordering a test.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBC, CRP, U&E, glucose, lactate, blood cultures — for sepsis and LRINEC)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guide resuscitation and support the diagnosis in hospital; must not delay surgery. Not obtainable via telehealth.
Confirmatory for fascial gas/necrosis but must NOT delay definitive surgery when clinical suspicion is high.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Doppler ultrasound to 'rule out DVT' before escalating
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Chasing a DVT scan in a septic patient with skin necrosis wastes time and endangers the limb and life — this is an error of delay, not a reasonable work-up.
